title: Zornia lutescens
taxonomy: Zornia lutescens is classified within the family Fabaceae, also known as the legume family. This classification is based on its anatomical and morphological characteristics, such as its compound leaves and pea-like flowers. Within the Fabaceae family, Zornia lutescens is further classified as part of the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Zornia is a small group of plants native to the Americas, with Zornia lutescens being one of the species found in the region. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships between Zornia lutescens and other related plant species.
uses: Zornia lutescens is known to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat different health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's roots and leaves are typically used to prepare remedies for these ailments. In some cultures, Zornia lutescens is also used to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

intro: Zornia lutescens, also known as Golden Button Clover, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zornia lutescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, colds, and digestive problems.
